name: Federal Reserve
summary: Rate at 3.5-3.75%. US March CPI 3.3% YoY with +0.9% monthly — biggest monthly since June 2022. Cleveland Fed warned rate hike possible. NY Fed flagged energy pass-through risk. Market pricing: 52% probability of Fed HIKE by year-end (first time >50%). PCE expected to hit 3.5% by April. Rate cut expectations that powered the ceasefire rally are DEAD — Islamabad failure + blockade persistence means oil stays elevated, inflation stays hot. April 29 hold 98.4%.

signals.content: FOMC voted 11-1 to hold rates at 3.50-3.75%. Projected one rate reduction in 2026 and another in 2027. But markets don't believe even one cut.
Market removed basically every rate cut for 2026, now pricing odds of a HIKE. Global bond yields at highest since May 2024. Pandemonium in bond markets.

Fed remains trapped — 10Y at 4.44%, oil at $112.57, VIX at 31.05. Can't cut (inflation), can't hike (growth risk). ECB froze rate cuts March 19. RBI holding at 5.25%. All three major central banks on hold = global monetary policy paralysis. $200B Pentagon supplemental adds fiscal pressure. Section 122 tariffs adding 0.5-0.6% to price level (Yale Budget Lab). Vance: war will continue 'a little while longer' — no near-term resolution to Fed's trap.

Stagflation trap deepening. Fed held at 3.5-3.75% (March 18). Market now pricing 52% probability of HIKE by year-end — first time crossing 50% threshold. Powell: inflation 'not coming down as hoped.' ECB expected to HIKE 25bp April and June (Goldman, ABN AMRO) — reversing easing path. Fed, ECB, and RBI ALL on hold simultaneously — global monetary policy paralysis. Oil shock prevents rate cuts that would otherwise support growth. CONFIRMED.

Eurozone inflation surged to 2.5% (Eurostat flash estimate), energy component exploded +4.9% (from -3.1% in February) — 8.0pp swing in one month. Most dramatic energy inflation print since 2022. Some analysts predict ECB rate HIKES at April and June meetings. Fed still trapped in stagflation: can't cut (oil above $100 = inflation), can't hike aggressively (growth risk). Pezeshkian peace signal briefly revived rate-cut conversation — if oil drops below $85, Fed can resume cuts. But Hormuz decoupling means oil may stay elevated even with ceasefire → Fed remains trapped. All three major central banks (Fed, ECB, RBI) frozen simultaneously. DXY dropped below 100 to 99.79 — dollar weakness despite elevated yields suggests market pricing growth concerns over inflation. (Sources: Eurostat, CNBC, Euronews)

Fed trapped in stagflation — ISM Prices Index at 78.3 (highest since June 2022, 19.3-point surge in 2 months) but employment contracting. 17 of 18 industries reported higher costs. Market pricing 52% probability of Fed HIKE by year-end for first time >50%. Bond market diverging from equity market — yields fell -2bps on peace day (pricing recession) while equities rallied (pricing de-escalation). Rate differential with ECB (3.5-3.75% vs 2.0%) persists. When bond and equity markets disagree, bonds are usually right.

DXY fell to 99.54 (-0.42%), below the psychologically significant 100 level. Dollar weakness despite elevated yields is a growth-concern signal, not an inflation-relief signal. Provides mechanical support for gold ($4,758), oil, and other dollar-denominated commodities. The DXY-below-100 regime is the most parsimonious explanation for gold + equities rallying simultaneously.

POST-ADDRESS: DXY surged back above 100 to 100.22 (+0.6%) after Trump 'stone ages' address. Oil +8.3% → inflation expectations spiked → safe-haven dollar flow → DXY above 100. The brief dip below 100 that mechanically supported gold is REVERSED. Gold crashed -3.1% to $4,636 as the oil→inflation→dollar→gold-down channel reactivated. US 10Y at 4.32% (+0.2%) — barely moved, pricing neither inflation relief nor recession conviction. VIX surged +11.4% to 27.34 — approaching 30 institutional threshold. (Sources: yfinance, TradingEconomics)

ISM Manufacturing Prices Index hit 78.3 — highest since June 2022, a 19.3-point surge in two months. Employment stays in contraction — textbook stagflation. Market pricing 52% probability of a Fed HIKE by year-end — first time above 50%. US 10Y yield at 4.30% — long end pricing growth deterioration. 2Y barely moved (3.61%) — short end anchored on policy. The bond-equity divergence: bonds pricing damage already baked in, equities celebrating peace headlines. Fed trapped same as RBI and ECB — can't cut (inflation), can't hike aggressively (growth). Oil at $109+ sustains the trap. (Sources: ISM, TradingEconomics, Bloomberg)

ISM Prices Index at 78.3 (highest since June 2022) foreshadows hot CPI print on April 10 — the next binary event for Fed policy. Oil back at $109-112 after deadline extension keeps inflation elevated. Fed trapped in stagflation: can't cut with oil-driven inflation running through 17 of 18 industries, can't hike without crashing equities already down 4-5% monthly. Market-implied hike probability holding at ~52%. US gasoline at $4.13/gallon national average — politically sensitive level. OPEC+ 206K bpd increase is symbolic (<2% of disrupted supply), confirming supply-side cannot rescue inflation. The April 10 CPI print will determine whether the 52% hike probability tips above or below the threshold — a hot print with oil at $109+ makes a June hike a live possibility. Gold at $4,783 futures (rising despite DXY above 100) reflects market's verdict that the Fed is paralyzed. (Sources: ISM, Trading Economics, Epoch Times, Barchart)

JP Morgan forecasts March CPI headline jumping from 2.4% to 3.4% YoY on 11% energy component — largest monthly jump since 2022 inflation spike. March NFP blowout: +178K vs +60K expected — strong jobs CLOSED rate-cut door. ISM Prices Index at 78.3 (highest since June 2022). ISM Employment contracting while NFP strong = textbook late-cycle divergence. 52% probability of Fed HIKE by year-end. Cleveland Fed inflation nowcasting tracking hot. April 10 CPI print will dominate mid-week — if above 3.4%, hike probability rises sharply. Stagflation comparisons to 1970s circulating. FOMC minutes also due this week. Fed in impossible position: hike into war-induced recession or let inflation run. (Sources: FinancialJuice, Cleveland Fed, yfinance)

March CPI release April 10 (T-3) — JP Morgan forecasts headline jumping from 2.4% to 3.4% YoY on 11% energy component. Largest monthly jump since 2022 inflation spike. ISM Prices Index at 78.3 = highest since June 2022. With WTI now $115/Brent $111 and the DXY holding 100.08, the dollar-strength paradox is feeding back into Fed policy expectations: rate hike probability rises if headline > 2.6%. The gold cooling to $4,685 from $4,796 record reflects exactly this — rising inflation expectations + rising rate expectations + DXY strength = compressed gold opportunity cost. Q1 earnings start April 10 (banks first); financials face credit concerns from war stress. The Fed has not commented publicly but the rate expectations curve has shifted hawkish.

EVENING UPDATE: Goldman raised US recession to 30% (3rd revision from 20→25→30). Moody's Zandi: 48.6% — 'recession more than likely by H2 unless hostilities end now.' Trigger: Brent $125 Q2 average = recession (currently $111, $14 gap). EY-Parthenon (Daco): 40%. Oxford Economics: world GDP 1.4% if war extends 6 months. Dimon (JPM letter): war may be 'defining factor' in global economic order. ISM Prices 78.3 = stagflation confirmation. Market pricing 52%+ hike probability on hot CPI. March CPI April 10 — JP Morgan forecasts 2.4→3.4% YoY. (Sources: Goldman Sachs, Moody's, EY-Parthenon, JPMorgan, Oxford Economics)

FED RECESSION-VIA-OIL PATHWAY MATERIALLY WEAKENED BY CEASEFIRE [REPORTED]. Pre-ceasefire: Goldman 30% / Moody's 48.6% recession probability driven primarily by Brent $115+ assumption. Post-ceasefire: Brent $94, Moody's Zandi 'Brent $125 Q2 average = recession' trigger now $30+ away from current spot. The recession-via-oil mechanism is materially weakened. US 10Y barely moved (4.34%, +0.2%) — bond market doesn't yet price big Fed dovish shift. US 2Y -0.2% (slight dovish nudge). DXY -1.1% — dollar relief rotation. The Fed's 'patient hold while oil-driven inflation passes through' framing is intact; hot CPI Friday is now less likely to force a hike. Watch Goldman/Moody's revised recession forecasts in next 24-48 hours.

Rate-cut probability nearly doubled post-ceasefire: from 25% to 43% for at least one cut in 2026 (CME FedWatch). Rate-hike odds halved from ~25% to 14%. FOMC March minutes (released April 8 evening) revealed hawkish tilt — several members discussed possibility of rate hikes if oil stayed elevated — but with WTI now ~$96, the inflation persistence argument weakened. Citigroup: if oil continues falling, sees potential for THREE cuts starting September. Oxford Economics: baseline TWO cuts to preempt labor market weakness. April 29 FOMC: 98% probability of no change. This is the ceasefire's most durable market legacy — it shifted the monetary policy distribution function. Friday April 10 March CPI release (consensus 3.2-3.4% vs Feb 2.4%) will test durability. (Source: CNBC, Benzinga, CME FedWatch, Citigroup, Oxford Economics)

March CPI RESOLVED at 3.3% YoY — 0.1pp BELOW 3.4% consensus [CONFIRMED, BLS official release]. Core CPI 2.6% YoY, +0.2% MoM — both 0.1pp below forecast. Energy +10.9%, gasoline +21.2% (largest monthly since 1967). Textbook oil-supply-shock print — core well-behaved, headline driven by single exogenous variable. Rate HIKE fears 'off the table' per FinancialContent [CONFIRMED]. CME FedWatch: June cut probability dropped from 55% to 35%, rate hike near zero, April 29 hold 98.4% [CONFIRMED, CME FedWatch]. For RBI: benign core means Malhotra's dovish June cut pathway survives — Fed won't hike. Market SEPARATED headline from core — treating 3.3% as transitory war-inflation. MOST DANGEROUS positioning: if Islamabad fails and oil returns to $110+, May CPI 3.5-4.0% collapses 'transitory' narrative.

US March CPI 3.3% YoY with +0.9% monthly — biggest monthly increase since June 2022. Cleveland Fed warned rate hike possible. NY Fed flagged energy pass-through risk. Market pricing: 52% probability of Fed HIKE by year-end (first time >50%). PCE expected to hit 3.5% by April on lagged oil pass-through. Islamabad failure means oil stays elevated → rate cut expectations that powered the ceasefire equity rally are dead. Bond-equity divergence: bonds validated.

edges.context: Rate-cut probability doubled post-ceasefire. 10Y barely moved (-5bps to 4.29%) — bond market skeptical. Friday CPI will test durability.
S&P +2.51% on ceasefire. VIX crushed to 21. Rate-cut probability shift is ceasefire's most durable equity-supportive legacy.
Gold regime shift CONFIRMED: DXY crashed to 98.58 on ceasefire → gold UP. Rate-cut probability doubled → dollar weakness → gold benefits. Gold is now a Fed/dollar hedge, not war hedge.
Oil crashed to $96 on ceasefire — below the $100 threshold. If sustained, Fed can resume cuts. But physical oil at $120+ means futures may snap back. Ceasefire fragility is the key variable.
Rate-cut probability shift is ceasefire's most durable legacy. FOMC March minutes hawkish but overtaken by events. DXY at 98.58.

trigger_points.mechanism: Would crash equities globally. EM currencies including INR would face massive outflows. Gold would paradoxically benefit as recession fears rise.
